item.title: How to make sense of SpaceX’s nonsensical valuation
item.description: The market has never before had to price a stock so speculative yet so large

item.title: Stellantis to invest €60bn and launch 60 new models under plan to revive carmaker
item.description: European group will partner with Chinese rivals and keep all of its factories under strategy laid out by chief executive

item.title: US to invest $2bn in nine quantum computing companies
item.description: Beneficiaries include start-up backed by firm with links to the Trump family

item.title: Closing tax loophole for oil groups will fund cost of living package, says Reeves
item.description: Chancellor sets out sweeping package of measures aimed at helping businesses and families

item.title: Retail investors turn to ‘options income’ ETFs in search for yield
item.description: Popularity of UK and European funds that use derivatives contracts to generate income soars in two years

item.title: Can AI make the public sector more efficient?
item.description: Productivity gains may be cancelled out by the public’s own use of the technology when interacting with authorities

item.title: Walmart profit growth slows as Iran war pushes up fuel costs
item.description: US retail giant said it absorbed additional costs to hold down prices for consumers

item.title: Is gas making a comeback on the US power grid?
item.description: Surging energy demand causes grid operators to prioritise fossil fuels

item.title: Berlin set to buy 40% stake in Franco-German tank maker KNDS
item.description: Decision clears path for defence group’s planned listing before summer

item.title: FirstFT: SpaceX lays out plan for biggest IPO in history
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Nvidia earnings and a profile of TCI’s Chris Hohn

item.title: Starling’s profits eroded by interest rate cuts as bank fails to diversify
item.description: Revenue and profits fall at UK neobank as it looks for acquisitions to push into lending and US market

item.title: The failure of global crisis management
item.description: Who really runs the world? The answer, nowadays, is nobody

item.title: Generating tax revenues in an automated world
item.description: If AI destroys job markets, governments will need to make up the resulting shortfall in labour income tax receipts

item.title: SpaceX awards bragging rights to Goldman Sachs
item.description: It may seem petty, but bankers are obsessed with who gets IPO top billing

item.title: Investec targets UK wealthy with private banking push
item.description: Group’s move set to intensify competition among lenders and fintechs chasing rich customers

item.title: AJ Bell boss warns of Isa reform ‘black hole’
item.description: Investment site says proposed rules could deter first-time investors

item.title: UK business activity fell for first time in more than a year in May
item.description: PMI figures reflect domestic political uncertainty adding to fallout from the Middle East war

item.title: EasyJet bumps up ticket prices to offset rise in jet fuel costs
item.description: Airline reports half-year loss as customers show hesitance to book amid ongoing conflict in Iran

item.title: BT loses fewer broadband customers than feared
item.description: Telecoms group expands cost-cutting target as turnaround continues

item.title: SpaceX’s China-less IPO and other astronomical figures
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Jamie Dimon praises China for being ‘more consistent’
item.description: JPMorgan chief says US is ‘still a safe haven’ even though it ‘surprised people recently’

item.title: HMRC vs KFC: Buying the dip
item.description: Somehow, the British taxman is once again in an argument about chicken

item.title: The ECB’s window to ‘look through’ the energy shock has already shut
item.description: Read the May forecast from the FT’s Monetary Policy Radar team

item.title: Chris Hohn’s quest for eternal greatness
item.description: The UK’s most successful hedge fund manager has taken a spiritual turn

item.title: The Peckham shopping centre in the middle of Britain’s fight to build new homes
item.description: Developer Berkeley Group says rejected Aylesham plan is example of why housebuilders can “no longer invest” in the UK capital

item.title: Saudi Arabia stops new work for consultants as war rattles finances
item.description: Kingdom delays payments amid attempts to tighten controls on spending for Vision 2030 megaprojects

item.title: Big Tech software era is over, says top investor James Anderson
item.description: Former Baillie Gifford fund manager says spoils of AI war will flow to hardware suppliers

item.title: DR Congo races to halt spread of Ebola
item.description: Reporting breakdown and weak health systems in conflict-hit country allowed virus to go undetected for weeks

item.title: Europe’s best employers 2026: ranking
item.description: Employees across the continent were surveyed by the FT and Statista for this edition of the list

item.title: Starmer’s crisis lands on UK housebuilders’ doorstep
item.description: The sector is not in crisis but does face a cyclical slowdown, with a lot of hot air already coming out of stock prices
